blocks.heading: ENGINEERING EVALUATION
blocks.text:
ENGINEERING EVALUATION
N  
 
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
150 Solano Way, Martinez, CA 94553  
Plant  No. 14628 
Application No. 30806 
 
This document is the Air District’s Engineering Evaluation Report for the Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company 
LLC application for a change of condition to establish an Alternate NO x Compliance Plan , Application No. 30806 . It 
describes how the proposed changes satisfy  Air District regulatory requirements for the issuance of a Permit to 
Operate , and it proposes permit conditions  to ensure compliance.  
 
1.0 - Background  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC, an indirect, wholly -owned  subsidiary of Marathon Petroleum 
Corporation (herein referenced as “Marathon”), has applied  for a change of condition to the Permit to Operate for 
the following equipment to comply with Regulation 9, Rule 10, Section 308:  
 S-919 No. 2 HDS Depent  Reboiler (F19); 65 MM Btu/hr 
S-920 No. 2 HDS Charge Heater (F20); 63 MMBtu/hr  
S-926 No. 2 Reformer Splitter Reboiler (F26) ; 130 MMBtu/hr  
S-928  HDN Reactor A Heater (F28); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-929  HDN Reactor B Heater (F29); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-930  HDN Reactor C Heat er (F30); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-931  Hydrocracker Reactor 1 Heater (F31); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-932  Hydrocracker Reactor 2 Heater (F32); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-933  Hydrocracker Reactor 3 Heater (F33); 20 MMBtu/hr  
S-934  Hydrocracker Stabilizer Reboiler (F34); 135 MMBtu/hr  
S-935 Hydrocracker Splitter Reboiler (F35) ; 135 MMBtu/hr  
S-937  Hydrogen Plant Heater (F37); 743 MMBtu/hr  
S-973 No. 3 HDS Recycle Gas Heater (F55 ); 110 MMBtu/hr  
 
Marathon operates 29 combustion devices constructed before 1994 that are subject to Air District  Regulation 9 -
10-301, which limits the refinery -wide emission rate from boilers, steam generators, and process heaters to 0.033 
pounds of nitrogen oxide (NO
x) per million Btu of heat input  (0.033 lbs/MMBtu), based on an operating day 
average.  
 Marathon Martinez Refinery has been idled since April 2020, i.e., the combustion devices associated with this 
application are not in operation at the time of this permit application. Marathon has submitted Application No. 
30768 to convert the existing Martinez Refinery from its current production of fossil fuels (i.e., conventional diesel fuel, gasoline, distillates, propane, and various by- products) to the production of ren ewable fuels, including 
renewable diesel, renewable propane, and renewable naphtha. In the past, Marathon was able to comply with the 0.033 lbs/MMBtu limit based on actual firing rates and NO
x emissions from continuous emission monitoring 
system (CEMS) measurements and operate units without a NO x CEMS within established NO x box parameters 
(Permit Condition #18372, Part 31).   
 
As part of Marathon’s Renewable Fuels Project (Application No. 30768) , Marathon has submitted Device Data 
Update Forms (dated 8/30/2022 and 10/14/2022) to the Air District to shut down 16 of the 29 combustion devices. Of the remaining 13 combustion devices, Marathon is expecting to exceed the activity -based emission limit 
although the total amount of NO
x emissions is expected to decrease.  
 
In 2013, the Air District amended Regulation 9- 10, Nitrogen Oxides and Carbon Monoxide from Boilers, Steam 
Generators and Process Heaters in Refineries, to include an Alternate NO x Compliance Plan (ANCP) option that 
provided for an alternative standard for older units based on mass emissions while limiting the heaters to their 
 
 Application No. 30806 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 2  of 15  
 historic NO x mass emission levels. A refinery wishing to meet a mass- based NO x standard for its older units can 
submit continuous emission monitoring system (CEMS) data or, in the absence of CEMS data, source test data 
showing daily mass emissions for 10 days of operation of the units during the prior three years, with the same 10 
days used for all units. The Ai r District reviews the data to verify that the data meets rule requirements. If the data 
is adequate, the rule allows the establishment of  a daily mass emission limit  that is the average of the 10 daily 
mass emission measurements.  
 With this application, Marathon is proposing to establish a daily NO
x mass emissions limit to comply with 
Regulation 9 -10 pursuant to Regulation 9 -10-308 as an ANCP, effective December 1, 2022. The Air District will 
impose the alternate refinery -wide daily  NO X mass emissions limit, in pounds NO x/day, in lieu of the activity- based 
emissions limit of 0. 033 lbs/MMBtu in Regulation 9- 10-301. To establish the alternate refinery -wide daily NO x mass 
emissions, Marathon has provided data to establish the  baseline N Ox daily emissions for each device based on the 
average of the daily emissions on any ten (10) different days during the 3- year period immediately preceding the 
date of the application, on which the refinery operator was in compliance with Regulation 9 -10-301.  
 
In addition, Marathon is proposing to install NO x CEMS on S -928, S -929, S -930, S -931, S -932, and S- 933 to comply 
with compliance monitoring requirements pursuant to Regulation 9- 10-502, which requires the owner/operator of 
boilers, steam generators, or process heaters subject to Regulation 9 -10-301 or 9-10 -308 to monitor at least 95% 
of the NO x emissions, by weight, with a NO x CEMS.  Marathon is proposing to install one CEMS for S -928 thru S- 933, 
which share a duct to the stack, and relocate the test port of the NO x CEMS for S -934. On June 8, 2022, the Air  
District’s Source Test Section approved the CEMS installation and duct configuration. The CEMS will be installed 
according to the approved monitoring plan , the Air District’s Manual of Procedures, Volume V and EPA Method 1.  
 
Upon implementation of the ANCP, 100% of the NO x emissions will be monitored using a NO x CEM , thereby 
eliminating the  requirement s of operating within the NOx box ranges (Permit Condition #18372, Part 31) and 
source test requirement s for using parametric monitoring system to monitor compliance with Regulation 9 -10-301 
or 308 (Permit Condition #18372, Part 33).     
 
2.0 Emissions Calculation  
As required by Regulation 9- 10-308.1, Marathon submitted data for 10 different days during the 3- year period 
immediately preceding the date of this application  (received October 22, 2020). As shown, the 10 days selected are 
all in compliance with the activity -based NO x emission limit of 0.033 lbs/MMBtu pursuant to Regulation 9- 10-301.  
 
Table 1 – Baseline Dates for ANCP 
Day Date  Daily NO x Emission 
Rate (lbs/ MMB tu) 
1 11/28/2017 0.030  
2 12/16/2017 0.031  
3 12/22/2017  0.031  
4 12/23/2017  0.031  
5 12/24/2017  0.030  
6 12/25/2017  0.030  
7 12/26/2017  0.030  
8 12/27/2017  0.030  
9 12/28/2017  0.030  
10 12/30/2017 0.030  
 
The sources listed in Table 2 are currently subject to the Regulation 9 -10-301 activity- based emissions limit. With 
the approval of this application, these sources will be subject to the alternate daily NO x mass emissions limit in 
accordance with Regulation 9 -10-308 (Permit Condition #18372, Part 37). All the sources listed in Table 2 are
